8282.text: The priority of an order of execution, the validity of which has been extended under Rule 48.45, shall be determined by reference to the date on which it was originally delivered to the relevant enforcement officer

8284.text: The production of a writ of execution , or of such a notice as is mentioned in Rule 48.47 purporting in either case to be sealed as mentioned in that Rule, shall be evidence that the validity of that order of execution, or, as the case may be, of the order referred to in that notice, has been extended under Rule 48.45.

8286.text: Subject to Rule 48.46, if during the validity of an order of execution, an interpleader claim is issued in relation to an execution under that order, the validity of the order shall be extended until the expiry of 12 months from the conclusion of the interpleader proceedings.

8289.text: Any party at whose instance or against whom an order of execution was issued, may serve a notice on the relevant enforcement officer requiring him, within such time as may be specified in the notice, to indorse on the order a statement of the manner in which he has executed it and to send to that party a copy of the statement.

8291.text: If an enforcement officer on whom such a notice is served fails to comply with it, the party by whom it was served may apply to the Court for an order directing the enforcement officer to comply with the notice.

8292.text: Power To Stay Execution Against Assets

8294.text: Where a judgment is given or an order made for the payment by any person of money, and the Court is satisfied, on an application made at the time of the judgment or order , or at any time thereafter, by the judgment debtor or other party liable to execution:

8295.text: (1) that there are special circumstances which render it inexpedient to enforce the judgment or order ; or

8296.text: (2) that the applicant is unable from any cause to pay the money;

8297.text: then, the Court may by order stay the execution against assets of the judgment or order either absolutely or for such period and subject to such conditions as the Court thinks fit.

8299.text: An application under Rule 48.53, if not made at the time the judgment is given or order made, must be made in accordance with Part 23, and may be so made notwithstanding that the party liable to execution did not acknowledge service of the claim form or serve a defence or take any previous part in the proceedings.

8301.text: The grounds on which an application under Rule 48.53 is made must be set out in the application notice and be supported by an affidavit made by or on behalf of the applicant substantiating the grounds and, in particular, where the application is made on the grounds of the applicant’s inability to pay, disclosing his income, the nature and value of any property of his and the amount of any other liabilities of his.

8303.text: The application notice and a copy of the supporting affidavit must be served on the party entitled to enforce the judgment or order not less than 4 clear days before the hearing.

8305.text: An order staying execution under Rule 48.53 may be varied or revoked by a subsequent order.

8306.text: Separate Orders To Enforce Payment Of Costs

8308.text: Where only the payment of money, together with costs to be assessed in accordance with Part 40 (detailed costs assessment), is adjudged or ordered, then, if when the money becomes payable under the judgment or order the costs have not been assessed, the party entitled to enforce that judgment or order may issue a writ for execution against assets to enforce payment of the sum (other than for costs) adjudged or ordered and, not less than 8 days after that order is made, he may obtain a second writ to enforce payment of the assessed costs.

8310.text: A party entitled to enforce a judgment or order for the delivery of possession of any property (other than money) may, if he so elects, obtain a separate writ to enforce payment of any damages or costs awarded to him by that judgment or order .

8311.text: Order For Sale Otherwise Than By Auction

8313.text: An order of the Court under Article 45(3) of the Law of Damages emedies 2005 that a sale of goods seized under an execution may be made otherwise than by public auction may be made on the application of:

8314.text: (1) the judgment creditor ; (2) the judgment debtor ; or

8317.text: An application under Rule 48.60 must be made in accordance with Part 23 and the application notice must contain a short statement of the grounds of the application.

8319.text: Where the applicant for an order under this Rule 48.60 is not the enforcement officer , the enforcement officer must, on the demand of the applicant, send to the applicant a list stating:

8320.text: (1) whether he has notice of the issue of another writ or writs of execution against the goods of the judgment debtor ; and

8321.text: (2) so far as is known to him, the name and address of every creditor who has obtained the issue of another writ of execution ,

8322.text: and where the enforcement officer is the applicant, he must prepare such a list.

8324.text: Not less than 4 clear days before the hearing, the applicant must serve the application notice on each of the other persons by whom the application might have been made and on every person named in the list under Rule 48.62.

8326.text: The applicant must produce the list under Rule 48.62 to the Court on the hearing of the application.

8328.text: Every person on whom the application notice was served may attend and be heard on the hearing of the application.

8329.text: PART 49 Court’s Power To Appoint A Receiver

8331.text: Scope Of This Part And Interpretation

8332.text: 49.1 This Part contains provisions about the Court’s power to appoint a receiver .

8334.text: In this Part ‘receiver’ includes a manager.

8337.text: The Court’s powers to appoint a receiver are set out in:

8340.text: The Court may appoint a receiver :

8341.text: (1) before proceedings have started; (2) in existing proceedings; or (3) on or after judgment .

8343.text: The Court will normally only consider an application for the appointment of a receiver before proceedings are started after notice of the application has been served.

8345.text: Where a judgment creditor applies for the appointment of a receiver as a method of enforcing a judgment , in considering whether to make the appointment the Court will have regard at all times to:

8346.text: (1) the sum owed; (2) the likelihood of making a substantial recovery; and (3) the cost of appointing the receiver .

8348.text: The Court may at any time:

8349.text: (1) terminate the appointment of a receiver ; and (2) appoint another receiver in his place.

8350.text: How To Apply For The Appointment Of A Receiver

8352.text: An application for the appointment of a receiver :

8353.text: (1) may be made without notice; and (2) must be supported by written evidence.

8355.text: If a person applies at the same time for:

8356.text: (1) the appointment of a receiver ; and

8358.text: he must use the same claim form or application notice for both applications.

8360.text: The written evidence in support of an application for the appointment of a receiver must:

8361.text:
(1) explain the reasons why the appointment is required;   
(2) give details of the property which it is proposed that the receiver should get in or manage, including estimates of:

8362.text: (a) the value of the property; and (b) the amount of income it is likely to produce;

8363.text: (3) if the application is to appoint a receiver by way of equitable execution, give details of:

8364.text: (a) the judgment which the applicant is seeking to enforce; (b) the extent to which the debtor has failed to comply with the judgment ; (c) the result of any steps already taken to enforce the judgment ; and (d) why the judgment cannot be enforced by any other method; and

8365.text: (4) if the applicant is asking the Court to allow the receiver to act:

8366.text: (a) without giving security; or (b) before he has given security or satisfied the Court that he has security in place; explain the reasons why that is necessary.

8368.text: In addition, the written evidence should normally identify an individual whom the Court is to be asked to

8369.text: appoint as receiver (‘the nominee’), and should:

8370.text: (1) state the name, address and position of the nominee; (2) include written evidence by a person who knows the nominee, stating that he believes the nominee is a suitable person to be appointed as receiver , and the basis of that belief; and (3) be accompanied by written consent, signed by the nominee, to act as receiver if appointed.

8372.text: If the applicant does not nominate a person to be appointed as receiver , or if the Court decides not to appoint the nominee, the Court may:

8373.text: (1) order that a suitable person be appointed as receiver ; and (2) direct any party to nominate a suitable individual to be appointed.

8375.text: A party directed to nominate a person to be appointed as receiver must file written evidence containing the information required by Rule 49.11 and accompanied by the written consent of the nominee.

8378.text: An order appointing a receiver must be served by the party who applied for it on:

8379.text:
(1) the person appointed as receiver ;   
(2) unless the Court orders otherwise, every other party to the proceedings; and (3) such other persons as the Court may direct.

8381.text: 49.15 The Court may give directions to the receiver when it appoints him or at any time afterwards.

8383.text: The Court will normally, when it appoints a receiver , give directions in relation to security.

8385.text: Other matters about which the Court may give directions include:

8386.text:
(1) whether, and on what basis, the receiver is to be remunerated for carrying out his functions;   
(2) the preparation and service of accounts;

8387.text: (3) the payment of money into Court ; and (4) authorising the receiver to carry on an activity or incur an expense.

8390.text: The Court may direct that before a receiver begins to act or within a specified time he must either:

8391.text: (1) give such security as the Court may determine; or (2) file and serve on all parties to the proceedings evidence that he already has in force sufficient security; to cover his liability for his acts and omissions as a receiver .

8393.text: An order appointing a receiver will normally specify the date by which the receiver must:

8394.text: (1) give security; or (2) file and serve evidence to satisfy the Court that he already has security in force.

8396.text: Unless the Court directs otherwise, security will be given by a guarantee.

8398.text: Where the Court has given directions about giving security, then a guarantee should be prepared in a form, and entered into with a bank or insurance company, approved by the Court .

8400.text: The Court may terminate the appointment of the receiver if he fails to:

8401.text:
(1) give the security; or   
(2) satisfy the Court as to the security he has in force, and (3) by the date specified.

8404.text: The receiver may apply to the Court at any time for directions to assist him in carrying out his function as a receiver .

8406.text: The Court , when it gives directions, may also direct the receiver to serve on any person:

8407.text: (1) the directions; and (2) the application for directions.

8409.text: An application by a receiver for directions may be made by filing an application notice in accordance with Part 23.

8411.text: If the directions sought by the receiver are unlikely to be contentious or important to the parties, he may make the application by letter, and the Court may reply by letter. In such cases the receiver need not serve his letter or the Court’s reply on the parties, unless the Court orders him to do so.

8413.text: Where a receiver applies for directions by letter, the Court may direct him to file and serve an application notice .

8416.text: A receiver may only charge for his services if the Court:

8417.text: (1) so directs; and (2) specifies the basis on which the receiver is to be remunerated.

8420.text: (1) who is to be responsible for paying the receiver ; and (2) the fund or property from which the receiver is to recover his remuneration.

8422.text: If the Court directs that the amount of a receiver’s remuneration is to be determined by the Court:

8423.text: (1) the receiver may not recover any remuneration for his services without a determination by the Court ; and

8424.text: (2) the receiver or any party may apply at any time for such a determination to take place.

8426.text: Unless the Court orders otherwise, in determining the remuneration of a receiver the Court shall award such sum as is reasonable and proportionate in all the circumstances and which takes into account:

8429.text: An application by a receiver for the amount of his remuneration to be determined must be supported by:

8431.text: (a) on what basis the remuneration is claimed; and (b) that it is justified and in accordance with this Part; and

8432.text: (2) a certificate signed by the receiver that he considers that the remuneration he claims is reasonable and proportionate.

8434.text: The Court may, before determining the amount of a receiver’s remuneration:

8435.text: (1) require the receiver to provide further information in support of his claim; and (2) appoint an assessor under Part 31 to assist the Court .

8437.text: The Court may refer the determination of a receiver’s remuneration to the Registrar.

8439.text: Rules 49.28 to 49.34 do not apply to expenses incurred by a receiver in carrying out his functions. These are accounted for as part of his account for the assets he has recovered, and not dealt with as part of the determination of his remuneration.

8442.text: The Court may order a receiver to prepare and serve accounts.

8444.text: A party served with such accounts may apply for an order permitting him to inspect any document in the possession of the receiver relevant to those accounts.

8446.text: A party should not apply for an order under Rule 49.37 without first asking the receiver to permit inspection without an order.

8448.text: Where the Court makes an order under Rule 49.37, it will normally direct that the receiver must:

8449.text: (1) permit inspection within 7 days after being served with the order; and

8450.text: (2) provide a copy of any documents the subject of the order within 7 days after receiving a request for a copy from the party permitted to inspect them, provided that party has undertaken to pay the reasonable cost of making and providing the copy.

8452.text: Any party may, within 14 days of being served with the accounts, serve notice on the receiver :

8453.text: (1) specifying any item in the accounts to which he objects;

8454.text: (2) giving the reason for such objection; and

8455.text: (3) requiring the receiver , within 14 days of receipt of the notice, either:

8456.text: (a) to notify all the parties who were served with the accounts that he accepts the objection; or

8457.text: (b) if he does not accept the objection, to apply for an examination of the accounts in relation to the contested item.

8459.text: When the receiver applies for the examination of the accounts he must at the same time file:

8461.text: (2) a copy of the notice served on him under Rule 49.40.

8463.text: If the receiver fails to comply with Rule 49.40(3), any party may apply to the Court for an examination of the accounts in relation to the contested item.

8465.text: At the conclusion of its examination of the accounts the Court will certify the result.

8467.text: When the Court gives directions under Rule 49.36 for the receiver to prepare and serve accounts, it may:

8468.text: (1) direct the receiver to prepare and serve accounts either by a specified date or at specified intervals; and

8469.text: (2) specify the persons on whom he must serve the accounts.

8471.text: A party should not apply for an order under Rule 49.37 permitting him to inspect documents in the possession of the receiver , without first asking the receiver to permit such inspection without an order.

8474.text: If a receiver fails to comply with any Rule, Practice Direction or direction of the Court the Court may order him to attend a hearing to explain his non-compliance.

8476.text: At the hearing the Court may make any order it considers appropriate, including:

8477.text: (1) terminating the appointment of the receiver ; (2) reducing the receiver’s remuneration or disallowing it altogether; and (3) ordering the receiver to pay the costs of any party.

8480.text: (1) the Court has ordered a receiver to pay a sum of money into Court ; and

8481.text: (2) the receiver has failed to do so;

8482.text: the Court may order him to pay interest on that sum for the time he is in default at such rate as it considers appropriate.

8485.text: A receiver or any party may apply for the receiver to be discharged on completion of his duties.

8487.text: The application notice must be served on the persons who were required under Rule 49.14 to be served with the order appointing the receiver .

8488.text: Order Discharging Or Terminating Appointment Of Receiver

8490.text: An order discharging or terminating the appointment of a receiver may:

8491.text: (1) require him to pay into Court any money held by him; or (2) specify the person to whom he must pay any money or transfer any assets still in his possession; and (3) make provision for the discharge or cancellation of any guarantee given by the receiver as security.

8493.text: The order must be served on the persons who were required under Rule 49.14 to be served with the order appointing the receiver .

8494.text: PART 50 Orders To Obtain Information From Judgment Debtors

8496.text: Scope Of This Part And Interpretation

8498.text: This Part contains rules which provide for a judgment debtor to be required to attend Court to provide information, for the purpose of enabling a judgment creditor to enforce a judgment or order against him.

8501.text: A judgment creditor may apply for an order requiring:

8502.text: (1) a judgment debtor ; or (2) if a judgment debtor is a company or other corporation, an officer of that body; to attend Court to provide information about: (a) the judgment debtor’s means; or

8503.text: (b) any other matter about which information is needed to enforce a judgment or order .

8505.text: An application under Rule 50.2 may be made without notice.

8507.text: The application must be made by filing an application notice in Form P50/01 if the application is to question an individual judgment debtor , or Form P50/02 if the application is to question an officer of a company or other corporation.

8510.text: (1) state the name and address of the judgment debtor ;

8511.text: (2) identify the judgment or order which the judgment creditor is seeking to enforce;

8512.text: (3) if the application is to enforce a judgment or order for the payment of money , state the amount presently owed by the judgment debtor under the judgment or order ;

8513.text: (4) if the judgment debtor is a company or other corporation, state:

8514.text: (a) the name and address of the officer of that body whom the judgment creditor wishes to be ordered to attend Court ; and

8516.text: (5) if the judgment creditor wishes the questioning to be conducted before a Judge, state this and give his reasons;

8517.text: (6) if the judgment creditor wishes the judgment debtor (or other person to be questioned) to be ordered to produce specific documents at Court , identify those documents; and

8518.text: (7) if the application is to enforce a judgment or order which is not for the payment of money, identify the matters about which the judgment creditor wishes the judgment debtor (or officer of the judgment debtor ) to be questioned.

8520.text: An application under Rule 50.2 may be dealt with by the Registrar without a hearing.

8522.text: If the application notice complies with Rules 50.4 and 50.5, an order to attend Court will be issued in the terms of Rule 50.9.

8525.text: (1) may, in any appropriate case, refer an application under Rule 50.2 to a Judge ; and

8526.text: (2) will refer it to a Judge for consideration, if the judgment creditor requests the judgment debtor (or officer of the judgment debtor ) to be questioned before a Judge .

8528.text: A person served with an order issued under Rule 50.7 must:

8529.text:
(1) attend Court at the time and place specified in the order;   
(2) when he does so, produce at Court documents in his control which are described in the order; and (3) answer on oath such questions as the Court may require.

8531.text: The order will normally provide for questioning to take place before the Registrar . The order will provide for questioning to take place before a Judge only if the Judge considering the request decides that there are compelling reasons to make such an order.

8533.text: An order under Rule 50.7 will contain a notice in the following terms:

8534.text: “You must obey this order. If you do not, you may be fined for contempt of Court.”

8537.text: An order to attend Court must, unless the Court otherwise orders, be served personally on the person ordered to attend Court not less than 14 days before the hearing.

8539.text: Service of an order to attend Court for questioning must be carried out by the judgment creditor (or someone acting on his behalf).

8541.text: The judgment creditor must inform the Court not less than 7 days before the date of the hearing if he has been unable to serve the order to attend Court .

8544.text: A person ordered to attend Court may, within 7 days of being served with the order, ask the judgment creditor to pay him a sum reasonably sufficient to cover his travelling expenses to and from Court .

8546.text: The judgment creditor must pay such a sum if requested.

8549.text: The judgment creditor must file an affidavit or affidavits :

8550.text: (1) by the person who served the order giving details of how and when it was served;

8552.text: (a) the person ordered to attend Court has not requested payment of his travelling expenses; or

8553.text: (b) the judgment creditor has paid a sum in accordance with such a request; and

8554.text: (3) stating how much of the judgment debt remains unpaid.

8557.text: (1) file the affidavit or affidavits not less than 2 days before the hearing; or (2) produce it or them at the hearing.

8560.text: The person ordered to attend Court will be questioned on oath.

8562.text: The questioning will be carried out by the Registrar unless the Court has ordered that the hearing shall be before a Judge .

8564.text: The Registrar will ask a standard series of questions, as set out forms in Schedules A and B to this Part. The form in Schedule A will be used if the person being questioned is the judgment debtor , and the form in Schedule B will be used if the person is an officer of a company or other corporation.

8566.text: The judgment creditor or his representative may either:

8567.text: (1) attend Court and ask questions himself or through his legal representative ; or

8568.text: (2) request the Registrar to ask additional questions, by attaching a list of proposed additional questions

8572.text: (1) make a written record of the evidence given, unless the proceedings are tape recorded;

8573.text: (2) at the end of the questioning, read the record of evidence to the person being questioned and ask him to sign it; and

8574.text: (3) if the person refuses to sign it, note that refusal on the record of evidence.

8576.text: Where the hearing takes places before a Judge , the judgment creditor or his legal representative must attend and conduct the questioning, and the standard questions in the forms in Schedules A and B will not be used.

8578.text: Where the hearing takes places before a Judge , the proceedings will be tape recorded and the Court will not make a written record of the evidence.

8581.text: If the hearing is adjourned, the Court will give directions as to the manner in which notice of the new hearing is to be served on the judgment debtor .

8584.text: Where a judgment debtor was ordered to attend before the Registrar , if a person against whom an order has been made under Rule 50.7:

8585.text: (1) fails to attend Court ; (2) refuses at the hearing to take the oath or to answer any question; or (3) otherwise fails to comply with the order; the Registrar will refer the matter to a Judge .

8587.text: If the Registrar refers to a Judge the failure of a judgment debtor to comply with an order under Rule 50.7, he will certify in writing the respect in which the judgment debtor failed to comply with the order.

8589.text: That Judge may, subject to Rules 50.31 and 50.32, make a committal order against the person.

8591.text: Where a judgment debtor was ordered to attend before a Judge , if a person against whom an order has been made under Rule 50.7:

8592.text: (1) fails to attend Court ; (2) refuses at the hearing to take the oath or to answer any question; or (3) otherwise fails to comply with the order; the Judge may, subject to Rules 50.31 and 50.32, make a committal order against the person.

8594.text: A committal order for failing to attend Court may not be made unless the judgment creditor has complied with Rules 50.15 to 50.18.

8596.text: If a committal order is made, the Judge will direct that:

8597.text: (1) the order shall be suspended provided that the person:

8598.text: (a) attends Court at a time and place specified in the order; and (b) complies with all the terms of that order and the original order; and

8599.text: (2) if the person fails to comply with any term on which the committal order is suspended, he shall be brought before a Judge to consider whether the committal order should be discharged.

8601.text: Where Rule 50.32 applies, the appointment specified will be:

8602.text:
(1) before a Judge , if: (a) the original order under Rule 50.7 was to attend before a Judge ; or (b) the Judge making the suspended committal order so directs; and   
(2) otherwise, before the Registrar .

8604.text: Rules 50.12 to 50.14 (service of order), and Rules 50.17(1) and 50.18 (affidavit of service ), apply with the necessary changes to a suspended committal order as they do to an order to attend Court .

8610.text: (1) ‘enforcement officer’ means the Court Bailiff or an individual who is authorised to act as such by the Chief Justice or a person acting on his behalf;

8611.text: (2) ‘order of execution’ includes an order of execution against assets, an order of possession, an order of delivery, or an order of sequestration and any further order in aid of any of the aforementioned orders.

8612.text: Entitlement To Relief By Way Of Interpleader

8615.text: (1) a person is under a liability in respect of a debt or in respect of any assets and he is, or expects to be, sued for or in respect of that debt or assets by two or more persons making adverse claims thereto; or

8616.text: (2) claim is made to any assets taken or intended to be taken by an enforcement officer in execution under any process, or to the proceeds or value of any such assets, by a person other than the person against whom the process is issued;

8617.text: the person under liability as mentioned in sub-paragraph (1) or (subject to Rules 51.3 to 51.6) an enforcement officer may apply to the Court for relief by way of interpleader.

8618.text: Claim To Assets Taken In Execution

8620.text: Any person making a claim to or in respect of any assets taken or intended to be taken in execution under process of the Court , or to the proceeds or value of any such assets, must give notice of his claim to the enforcement officer charged with the execution of the process and must include in his notice a statement of his address, and that address shall be his address for service .

8623.text: On receipt of a claim made under Rule 51.3 the enforcement officer must forthwith give notice thereof to the execution creditor and the execution creditor must, within 7 days after receiving the notice, give notice to the enforcement officer informing him whether he admits or disputes the claim. An execution creditor who gives notice in accordance with this Rule admitting a claim shall only be liable to the enforcement officer for any fees and expenses incurred by the enforcement officer before receipt of that notice.

8626.text: (1) the enforcement officer receives a notice from an execution creditor under Rule 51.4 disputing a claim, or the execution creditor fails, within the period mentioned in that Rule, to give the required notice; and

8627.text: (2) the claim made under Rule 51.3 is not withdrawn;

8628.text: the enforcement officer may apply to the Court for relief under this Part.

8630.text: An enforcement officer who receives a notice from an execution creditor under Rule 51.4 admitting a claim made under Rule 51.3 shall withdraw from possession of the assets claimed and may apply to the Court for an order restraining the bringing of a claim against him for or in respect of his having taken possession of the assets.

8631.text: Claim In Respect Of Assets Protected From Seizure

8633.text: Where a judgment debtor whose assets have been seized, or are intended to be seized, by an enforcement officer under an order of execution claims that such assets are not liable to execution by virtue of Article 45(2) of the Lawof Damages and Remedies 2005, he must within 5 days of the seizure give notice in writing to the enforcement officer identifying all those assets in respect of which he makes such a claim and the grounds of such claim in respect of each item.

8635.text: Upon receipt of a notice of claim under Rule 51.7, the enforcement officer must forthwith give notice thereof to the execution creditor and to any person who has made a claim to, or in respect of, the goods under Rule 51.3 and the execution creditor and any person who has made claim must, within 7 days of receipt of such notice, inform the enforcement officer in writing whether he admits or disputes the judgment debtor’s claim in respect of each item.

8637.text: The enforcement officer shall withdraw from possession of any goods in respect of which the judgment debtor’s claim is admitted or if the execution creditor or any person claiming under Rule 51.3 fails to notify him in accordance with Rule 51.8 and the enforcement officer shall so inform the parties in writing.

8639.text: Where the enforcement officer receives notice from:

8641.text: (2) any such person to whom notice was given under Rule 51.8;

8642.text: that the claim or any part thereof is disputed, he must forthwith seek the directions of the Court and may include therein an application for an order restraining the bringing of any claim against him for, or in respect of, his having seized any of those assets or his having failed so to do.

8644.text: The enforcement officer’s application for directions under Rule 51.10 shall be made by an application in accordance with Part 23 and, on the hearing of the application, the Court may:

8645.text: (1) determine the judgment debtor’s claim immediately; or

8646.text: (2) give such directions for the determination of any issue raised by such claim as may be just.

8648.text: The Registrar shall have power to make an order of the kind referred to in Rule 51.11.

8651.text: An application for relief under this Part must be made by claim form in accordance with Part 8 unless made in an existing claim, in which case it must be made in accordance with Part 23.

8653.text: Where the applicant is an enforcement officer who has withdrawn from possession of assets taken in execution and who is applying for relief under Rule 51.6 the claim form must be served on any person who made a claim under Rule 51.3 to or in respect of those assets, and that person may attend the hearing of the application.

8655.text: Subject to Rule 51.16 a claim form or application notice under Rule 51.13 must be supported by evidence that the applicant:

8656.text: (1) claims no interest in the subject matter in dispute other than for charges or costs;

8657.text: (2) does not collude with any of the claimants to that subject matter; and

8658.text: (3) is willing to pay or transfer that subject matter into Court or to dispose of it as the Court may direct.

8660.text: Where the applicant is an enforcement officer , he shall not provide such evidence as is referred to in Rule 51.15 unless directed by the Court to do so.

8662.text: Any person who makes a claim under Rule 51.3 and who is served with a claim form under Rule 51.14 shall within 14 days serve on the execution creditor and the sheriff an affidavit specifying and/or describing any assets claimed and setting out the grounds upon which such claim is based.

8664.text: Where the applicant is an enforcement officer a claim form under Rule 51.13 must give notice of the requirement in Rule 51.17.

8665.text: Powers Of Court Hearing The Claim

8667.text: Where on the hearing of a claim under this Part all the persons by whom adverse claims to the subjectmatter in dispute (hereafter in this Part referred to as ‘the interpleader claimants’) appear, the Court may order:

8668.text: (1) that any interpleader claimant be made a defendant in any claim pending with respect to the subject-matter in dispute in substitution for or in addition to the applicant for relief under this Part; or

8669.text: (2) that an issue between the interpleader claimants be stated and tried and may direct which of the interpleader claimants is to be claimants and which defendant .

8672.text: (1) the applicant under this Part is an enforcement officer ;

8673.text: (2) all the interpleader claimants consent or any of them so requests; or

8674.text: (3) the question at issue between the interpleader claimants is a question of law and the facts are not in dispute;

8675.text: the Court may immediately determine the question at issue between the interpleader claimants and make an order accordingly on such terms as may be just.

8677.text: Where an interpleader claimant, having been duly served with a claim form under this Part, does not appear at the hearing or, having appeared, fails or refuses to comply with an order made in the proceedings, the Court may make an order declaring the interpleader claimant, and all persons claiming under him, barred from prosecuting his claim against the applicant for such relief and all persons claiming under him, but such an order shall not affect the rights of the interpleader claimants as between

8679.text: Power To Order Sale Of Assets Taken In Execution

8681.text: Where an application for relief under this Part is made by an enforcement officer who has taken possession of any assets in execution under any process, and an interpleader claimant alleges that he is entitled to the assets by way of security for debt, the Court may order those assets or any part thereof to be sold and may direct that the proceeds of sale be applied in such manner and on such terms as may be just and as may be specified in the order.

8684.text: Where a defendant to a claim applies for relief under this Part in the claim, the Court may by order stay all further proceedings in the claim.

8687.text: The Court may in or for the purposes of any interpleader proceedings make such order as to costs or any other matter as it thinks just.

8689.text: Where the interpleader claimant fails to appear at the hearing, the Court may direct that the enforcement officer’s and execution creditor’s costs shall be assessed by the Registrar .

8692.text: Where the Court considers it necessary or expedient to make an order in any interpleader proceedings in several proceedings, the Court may make such an order; and the order shall bear the titles of all those proceedings and shall be binding on all the parties to them.

8695.text: Parts 19 and 28 shall, with the necessary modifications, apply in relation to an interpleader issue as they apply in relation to any other proceedings.
